header.title: The Gulf of Marseilles Seen from L'Estaque (ca. 1885) by Paul Cézanne. Original from The MET Museum. Digitally enhanced by rawpixel. (50639903857).jpg
header.description:
Free download under CC Attribution (CC BY 4.0). Please credit the artist and rawpixel.com. 
Paul Cézanne (1839–1906) was one of the greatest French painters of Post-Impressionism. He was known as the "Master of Aix" because of how he portrayed the beauty of nature in Aix-en-Provence, his ancestral home in the South of France. His artistic style of repetitive and exploratory brushstrokes made his works recognizable and paved the way for modern art and Cubism. Cezanne transitioned the art world an
header.author: Rawpixel Ltd
